points.name: Basic Point A — Left
points.shortDescription: Corresponds to the head and brain. Primary point for headache, neck pain, and neurological conditions.
points.longDescription: YNSA Basic Point A is located at the anterior hairline, approximately 1 cun lateral to the midline on the left side. It is the primary point for head and brain conditions in the Yamamoto system and is frequently used as the first point selected in treatment.

points.name: Basic Point A — Right
points.shortDescription: Corresponds to the head and brain. Mirror of Point A Left.
points.longDescription: The right-side counterpart to YNSA Basic Point A. Selection of left vs right is guided by diagnostic palpation of the neck diagnostic points (Ypsilon points).

points.name: Basic Point B — Left
points.shortDescription: Corresponds to the cervical spine and neck.
points.longDescription: YNSA Basic Point B is located slightly lateral to Point A at the anterior hairline. It primarily treats cervical spine conditions, neck stiffness, and whiplash injuries.

points.name: Basic Point B — Right
points.shortDescription: Corresponds to the cervical spine and neck. Mirror of Point B Left.
points.longDescription: The right-side counterpart to YNSA Basic Point B. Treats cervical spine conditions on the corresponding side as determined by diagnostic palpation.

points.name: Basic Point C — Left
points.longDescription: YNSA Basic Point C corresponds to the shoulder and is located further lateral along the anterior hairline. Effective for frozen shoulder, rotator cuff injuries, and shoulder impingement.

points.name: Basic Point C — Right
points.shortDescription: Corresponds to the shoulder joint. Mirror of Point C Left.
points.longDescription: YNSA Basic Point C corresponds to the shoulder and is located further lateral along the anterior hairline. Effective for frozen shoulder, rotator cuff injuries, and shoulder impingement.

points.name: Basic Point D — Left
points.longDescription: YNSA Basic Point D corresponds to the elbow. Located at the lateral hairline near the temple region. Used for tennis elbow, golfer's elbow, and other elbow conditions.

points.name: Basic Point E — Left
points.shortDescription: Corresponds to the wrist and hand.
points.longDescription: YNSA Basic Point E corresponds to the wrist. Located in the temporal region. Used for carpal tunnel syndrome, wrist sprains, and hand conditions.

points.name: Basic Point F — Left
points.longDescription: YNSA Basic Point F corresponds to the hip. Located in the temporal-parietal junction area. Used for hip osteoarthritis, hip replacement recovery, and hip pain.

points.name: Basic Point G — Left
points.longDescription: YNSA Basic Point G corresponds to the knee. Located in the parietal region. One of the most commonly used points for knee osteoarthritis, ACL injuries, and post-surgical knee rehabilitation.

points.name: Basic Point H — Left
points.shortDescription: Corresponds to the ankle and foot.
points.longDescription: YNSA Basic Point H corresponds to the ankle and foot. Located in the posterior parietal area. Used for ankle sprains, plantar fasciitis, and Achilles tendon conditions.
